problem_statement: Traditional 2D Tic-Tac-Toe is a solved game with optimal play always resulting in a draw. A 4×4×4 3D variant provides strategic depth while remaining visually intuitive.
solution_overview: A web-based 3D Tic-Tac-Toe game featuring a two-panel interface: four flat 4×4 grids for interaction and a 3D visualization for spatial understanding. Win detection covers all 76 possible lines.

must_have.name: 4x4x4 Board Data Model
must_have.description: 3D array representing the game board with 64 cells across 4 layers
acceptance_criteria: Board is represented as number[4][4][4] (z, y, x indexing)
Each cell can be empty (0), X (1), or O (2)
Immutable state updates on every move

must_have.name: Flat Board UI (Left Panel)
must_have.description: Four 4×4 grids rendered side-by-side, each representing one Z-layer
acceptance_criteria: 4 distinct grids labeled 'Layer 1 (bottom)' through 'Layer 4 (top)'
Cells are clickable to place marks
Visual indication of X vs O marks
Disabled cells for already-filled positions

must_have.name: 3D Visualization (Right Panel)
must_have.description: Three.js scene showing all 64 cells as a 4×4×4 cube structure
acceptance_criteria: Empty cells shown as transparent/outlined cubes
X cells shown as colored spheres (e.g., blue)
O cells shown as different colored spheres (e.g., red)
Camera orbits on drag (view-only, no cell placement)

must_have.name: Win Detection
must_have.description: Check all 76 possible winning lines after each move
acceptance_criteria: Detects wins along X, Y, Z axes (48 lines)
Detects wins along face diagonals (24 lines)
Detects wins along space diagonals (4 lines)
Returns winning player and the 4 cells forming the line

must_have.name: Win Highlighting
must_have.description: Visual highlight of winning cells in both UI panels
acceptance_criteria: Winning cells highlighted in flat grid view
Winning cells highlighted in 3D view
Winner banner displayed

must_have.name: Game Controls
must_have.description: Reset button and current player indicator
acceptance_criteria: Current player (X or O) clearly displayed
Reset button clears board and returns to player X
Game info panel shows turn count

nice_to_have.name: Piece Animation
nice_to_have.description: Animate placed pieces with scale-in effect in 3D view

nice_to_have.name: Ghost Preview
nice_to_have.description: Show a ghost preview on hover in flat grids

nice_to_have.name: Active Layer Highlight
nice_to_have.description: Highlight the active Z-layer when hovering over flat grid cells

usability: Game must be playable on desktop browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

success_metrics: All 76 winning line scenarios pass automated tests
Game is fully playable from start to win/draw
